title: verbena neomexicana
intro: Verbena neomexicana, also known as New Mexico verbena, is a perennial wildflower native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its small white or pink flowers and fragrant leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, making it effective in treating various health issues such as sore throats, wounds, and colds. The health benefits of Verbena neomexicana include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
